message:
fix(tests): resolve 3 Coverage Suite failures from PRs 9359 and 9361 (#9364)

Issue 9362 — Coverage Suite run #1199 flagged three post-merge test
regressions introduced by our own recent merges.

1. FeatureRequestModal > "closes cleanly after successful submission
   without prompting to save as draft" (from PR 9361)
   The test anchored on /Request Submitted/i, but the scoped
   react-i18next mock in this test file returns the i18n KEY when no
   fallback is provided. The SuccessView calls
   t('feedback.requestSubmitted') with no fallback, so the rendered
   text is the literal key "feedback.requestSubmitted", which does not
   match the regex. Anchor instead on the plain (non-i18n) paragraph
   "Your request has been submitted for review." that the SuccessView
   renders right below the heading — the assertion is still meaningful
   (the success view was reached) but no longer depends on the mock's
   key-returning behavior.

2+3. concurrent-mutation-safety ratchet (from PR 9359)
   The P2-B static scan flagged useCachedNodes.ts:
     L239: .push( on "collectedErrors" — collectedErrors.push({
   PR 9359 introduced a per-cluster error collector that was declared
   inside the fetcher but mutated from within the
   settledWithConcurrency callback — the exact pattern the ratchet
   catches (interleaved await writes to a shared accumulator).

   Refactor to the established "return values, aggregate after
   settlement" pattern used by the other 10 violations already fixed in
   6857. Each task now returns a tagged PerClusterNodeResult
   { nodes, error } tuple; the final node list and error list are built
   by settledResults.flatMap / .filter after settledWithConcurrency
   resolves. The module-level subscribable snapshot (used by
   useSyncExternalStore to bridge the fetcher-outside-React boundary)
   is unchanged — only the outer-scope mutation inside the callback is
   gone.

Verified locally:
  npx vitest run \
    src/components/feedback/FeatureRequestModal.test.tsx \
    src/test/concurrent-mutation-safety.test.ts \
    src/hooks/__tests__/useCachedNodes.test.ts \
    --no-coverage
  => 25 passed (all 3 targeted failures + existing useCachedNodes suite)

Fixes #9362

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
